reviews.review_msg: He slept during Bill Nye's speech. Best prof ever!!! Easy class
He is the best prof you can choose for Econ302.

494 is very beneficial to honor thesis by having more time to increase the quality of honor thesis. The two semesters of honor thesis give you more advantage when you are compared to other universities' students, especially when you apply external award for your paper to impress grad school. Steeve provided consistent feedback throughout semesters.
Final exam is super hardbut the course is curving so dont worry. Professor Steeve is the greatest prof I have met.
HARDEST FINAL EVER, the practice questions not related to final.
Lecture note are bit hard to understand, but Steeve is really helpful during his office hours. Studying lecture notes and tutorial questions are enough for the exams.

I really like Steeve's style. He is really a nice person to get along with, and available even outside announced office hours. He doesn't seem to have strict rules, at least in this course -- he understands and trusts students. The down side is his notes are a bit messy, you may want to use his office hour to ask him if anything is not clear.

Steeve is SUPER smart so sometimes you might be lost during his lectures, but he is extremely willing to help outside classes. Final was challenging but I like the originality of his questions. Really representative of the difficulty of masters coursework.
Steeve is passion and patient prof ever see before!!!! very nice guy, and likely use the easy understand example to explain complicated theory  and he understands what to teach and how to teach
WORST WORST WORST PROF EVER! Exams are so hard.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!!! HIS NOTES ARE USELESS AND WITH THAT AN EVEN MORE USELESS TEXTBOOK FULL OF ERRORS. Writes a bunch of algrebra that makes no sense, reading textbook just makes u even more confused. Doing questions from the book wont help u pass his 70% final

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS UNLESS YOU LOVE ALGEBRAIC MATH NOTES! He does no numerical examples in class, just messy chicken scratch algebraic math notes. 90% of my notes make no sense to me. Textbook sucks too. Overall terrible class! Dreading the 70% Final
Hard to understand, and his exams were a pain. Don't take this guy's class.

Best econ prof I've ever had. He designed the course well so that it's interesting and insightful and would  motivate students into looking at these fields/topics in the future by themselves. His markings r fair (based on understanding). he's down to earth and friendly that makes even shy students like me feel comfortable in asking questions.
great prof! take 103 w/ him!
Really Good Professor. Very straight forward. Exams were very fair, if the average is low the marks get inflated at the end. Awesome prof, take 103 with him.

His exams arn't ridiculously difficult. They make you think but I admit you do need some luck. Course is bellcurved. To do well you actually have to  understand and hope a bit of luck is on your side. I got an A  hope you can too.

this prof is beneath avg. His tests are hard,and not reflective of what he tells u to study, espicially the labs.Midterms had 25 multiple choice questions worth 4 marks each, all or nothing. Final exam had 5 T/F worth 8 points and had to write ur reasoning--HARD. But scales the exams a lot depending on class avg. Prof is French,wid POOR English.
lots of typos. need to answer the questions on every aspect u can think of...not just the question itself. Not much material to study for (which is a good thing)

he is the best prof ever!! So helpful and all his lectures are interesting and you learn so much!! It is hard at first but he is always ready to be of help!! He has funny comments also and is SOO pretty, lol like wow :)

he doesn't really have good structured lecture notes, usually all over the place, but he's very helpful during o/h's. m/t and final were on the hard side, but i think he's a pretty easy marker.  and he marks on a curve too...so that helps a bit.
i took econ 393 with him before and took 302 last semester. the two midterms were very hard. the class average was around 50. but the final was ok.

well..Lecures are easy. He spends a lot of time explaining the basics. The exams are unexpectable. Easy lecture notes don't help AT ALL. He seems easy going and cool but he'll stab you at the back in the exam! BE AWARE!
Best prof I have ever had! highly recommended
Very difficult final exam. Covered waaaaay too much material.
first midterm class average was 48%... sorta speaks for itself how "easy" he is eh?
Good enough class
No Comments
Good teacher, fair examiner. Open for extra help. Could make better, more clear exams though.

By far the best prof I have ever met. He is a prof that actually teach you stuff, and you actually learnging stuff in lecture unlike most of the other professors in SFU. He's very helpful and he will try his best to make sure his students to understand the concept. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!
nice prof
The best proff ever at <SFU !  easy to get a A plus...and he will give it to u ! take it fast !!
easy course ^^ fair MT.

Fair exam.
 Not need to read textbook.
 No homework
SUPER RECOMMENDED!
VERY NICE, HELPFUL AND HANDSOME PROFESSOR!!! RECOMMENDED!
classes are fun, gives lots of material, likes to give unsolvable stuff for the exams, but is an easy marker. try to make him say "household"
Not a very hard course, but you still need to understand how to apply models.  No text book was needed, just in class notes (saved me $$$$). I would take him as a prof again.
Easy class... I got an A! Lecture notes are not helpful, but you need to understand the material very well in order to get a good grade!Too bad this course is being offered only once a year
The most handsome prof. I've ever had.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
Nice looking professor.  Very nice and very helpful!!
midterm is trickly, easy notes can't help very much.
A very knowledgable and engaging lecturer! You MUST take his class!
he's the sharpest tool in the shed!
No Comments

What a hottie, easy on the eyes!
